title: Level 960
wikitext:
{{Audio|secrettext=RESIDUAL SPACES|file=Somethingsomething.mp3|loop=yes}}{{Background|file=Hallwaynew.jpg}}{{Dreamy Theme}}
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 960: "Maze of Concrete"}} {{Image Banner|image=A74375834457bbeffad7defd11c73d80.jpg|text=DRCAY}}{{Class Custom Image|class=Deadzone|hexagon=🕱|bg-image=Void.png|text-color=white|safety= Numerous Deadly Environmental Hazards|security= Highly Uninhabitable|entity=Presence of Several Lethal Entities}} =Level Description= '''Level 960''' or colloquially also known as '''"Maze of Concrete"''' or otherwise '''"Concrete Maze"''' is an infinite labyrinthine concrete maze consisting of non-Euclidean geometry. The maze is divided into 4 separate sub-layers, yet due to the level being highly uninhabitable, it is nearly impossible to measure if the sub-layers are sub-levels instead. Each area holds noteable differences, although they share the same common entities and objects, distinct from one another because of the color and their overall layout. Lights are ubiquitous in the drop-ceiling of the level, with nearly all of them being standard troffers. However, if a one tries to break the outer casing, one may get temporarily blinded because inside the fluorescent tubes turn out to be extremely bright, with most of the light being alleviated by the casing. Underneath the housing, the air inside of the plenum space is rendered toxic because accumulating inside is fatal amounts of bacteria, viruses, fungi, and potential remnants of the [[Hydrolitis Plague]]. It is possible for sporadically appearing areas filled with said contaminated air due to ceiling tiles that had come loose causing the air to seep out into the open, causing increased risk for a wanderer to be exposed to hazards. The most threatening part of the level is the concrete dust residing in the air of the level, consisting of a mix with respirable crystalline silica dust, hexavalent chromium, and calcium hydroxide. The dust seemingly affects respiration more than in other levels that include said compounds. It is shown that having exposure to the dust for more than 5 minutes in the level has shown a 70% increased risk of lung and respiratory system diseases especially silicosis and a numerous amount of skin issues, affecting the consumption of food and drinking. Exposure with the concrete dust has led to several cases of death on wanderers, with the larger percentage of wanderers who had left reportedly died predominantly due to lung disease. It is reported that every food or drink being left open, or in severe cases being simply left there, for surprisingly short periods of time, reportedly more than 3 minutes, leads to consumption having severe food poisoning and death as a side effect. The concrete dust in this level is theorized to have aided this phenomenon, although the reasoning for this still remains mostly unknown. It has been noticed that when a wanderer or an entity dies in '''Level 960''', the floors in the level seemingly "absorb" the body and clothes during a period of 40 minutes, leaving none to minimal trace of body. =Sub-Layers= ===Residual Space=== {{Blurimage|file=Hallwaynew.jpg|caption=A dated image of '''Level 960''' in '''Residual Space'''}} '''Residual Space''', or Sub-Layer 1, is the first portion in Level 960, and also the most lenient. '''Residual Space''' appears as an arrangement of white-concrete hallways with black-beige-rose patterned carpet flooring. '''Residual Space''' includes lesser labyrinthine architecture and integrates longer hallways. This is where wanderers typically first appear, though upon entry they invariably appear with [[Object 1|Almond Water]], a succulent and an empty photo frame atop a standard wooden table on their left. A perpetual creaking sound is heard, with the origin of the sound varying from place to place. On rare occasions, chairs, tables, and other wooden pieces of furniture are found here, but otherwise the area remains devoid of furniture and objects. Occasionally, entities may materialize out of cracks in the concrete seen here, albeit this only seldom happens because entities are an uncommon occurrence throughout. it has been reported that random objects in '''Residual Space''' randomly no-clip into seemingly nowhere and appear again at random intervals. Though not always, these objects when no-clipped can fall back into existence at dangerously high speeds, causing fractures in the structure and potentially hitting a wanderer. There are also objects that appear that do not correlate with whatever item had fell, and seem to come from an unknown location; of which spotted are: Miscellaneous electronics, furniture, food items, cans, appliances, and light bulbs. These objects often reappear once more but have subtle changes. The more the object repeats the cycle, the more distorted and modified they become. Occasionally, these objects may become stuck in the wall as well. The only two true confirmed exits are from vents and the area transitioning to the next, by wandering far will eventually transitioned to the second sub-layer. However, finding a vent is rare which means one may be stuck in the first layer for prolonged periods of time which may increase risks of an object striking them. ===The Decayed=== {{Blurimage|file=Dark hallway(3).jpg|caption=An image of '''The Decaying Maze'''}} '''The Decaying Maze''' is the second area and the area that encompasses the most of '''Level 960'''. Resembling the architecture of a decayed complex, the area includes an extremely large populace of black mold, unidentified fungi, bacteria, moss/algae and various microorganisms. The air is cluttered with aero plankton and bio aerosols. The air varies from a PM2.5 particulate matter to a PM10, although this only applies to its respective area. The area is dim and dark and usually claustrophobic making it hard to see, with the occasional flicking light. Sharing some similarities to the first area, it is extremely common for entities to materialize out of cracks in the wall. '''The Decaying Maze''' provides even more danger, with most of the walls being rotten and tearing off, which often sucks one to [[The Void]]. Objects can be found here via cabinets, seldom medicine cabinets even having proper medicine. It is not recommended for one to use this medicine as a lot of them are past expiration and have rotten. There is a perpetual ambiance of abnormal sounds including scratching, whispers, and screaming, often heard from inside of the walls. Where these sounds originate from are unknown, although they may come from entities or from audible hallucinations. There are puddles of bacteria-infested water, which includes the [[Hydrolitis Plague]]. There may be stairs leading downward to a basement-styled room, which on some occasions can be filled with bacteria-infested water. Ceiling can sometimes tear and open cracks and these cracks can often serve as an entity hotspot, which make a horde of entities fall atop or near wanderer. A wanderer can truly exit this area and the level as a whole by entering a crawlspace, leading one to [[Level 412]]. But to enter another sub-layer is to find a wall that has not decayed and get dragged into it, sending wanderers to the third sub-layer. ===The Concrete Office=== {{Blurimage|file=A74375834457bbeffad7defd11c73d80.jpg|caption=An image of '''The Concrete Office'''}} '''The Concrete Office''' is the third sub-layer in '''Level 960'''. It appears to be architecturally an office consisting of white painted concrete. This area is similar to '''The Decaying Maze''', but typically less darker and incorporates more open space. Objects are common in this area, one of these objects can be found on shelves bolted to doors, yet ,unusually, opening these types of doors always leads to a vast empty space devoid of furnishing, walls or objects. Entities are slightly commonly found here, and the most common type of entity are [[Entity 2|Windows]]. These [[Entity 2|Windows]] are almost always either bright or pitch black. If one of these [[Entity 2|Windows]] are bright, that means it is an entity and going near often leads to the wanderer being dragged in and never being seen again. If its black, it is either safe and normal or a window leading the wanderer to the [[The Void]]. Sometimes, one can hear water dripping. However, it is unknown where the dripping water originates from. it is theorized that it comes from hallucinations, with them being common in the level, though multiples reports have described the same thing which is seemingly abnormal. If the wanderer walks too deep in, the area may get even darker due to any lights present beyond at this point. Going too deep causes [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]] to be in place of [[Object 1|Almond Water]], although this wont affect almond water that the wanderer had brought. There is also reportedly [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]] puddles around '''The Concrete Office''', and can appear in darker areas on the floor, and reportedly seeping from inside the walls, leading to incidences of sudden death. '''The Concrete Office''' is also reported to have an entrenched scent of smoke, although it remains unknown where it comes from, and it can lead to secondary effects linked to bronchitis and miscellaneous lung diseases. The concrete dust here is less prominent. Rarely, is it spotted that the concrete is decaying. This sub-layer is relatively simple to escape by randomly no-clipping into the next, finding doors that simply lead to the next, or a gradual transition into the next occurring. But beware of [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]] due to reportedly seeping from inside the walls, which is dangerous if the wanderer makes contact with it. ===The Mental Concrete=== {{Blurimage|file=Hallwaynew2.jpg|caption=An image of '''The Mental Concrete'''}} '''The Mental Concrete''' is the fourth and last of the areas in '''Level 960'''. The location led of wanderers are led from random no-clipping in all three of '''The Decaying Maze''', and '''The Concrete Office'''. '''The Mental Concrete''', though entry from layer 1 & 2 are rarer. The area appears to be to made of pink-painted concrete on all walls, resembling an institution. The majority of lights are out of service, with the ones in service almost always having a white plastic door neath. Opening the door, however, is a seemingly insurmountable task. For doors that appear without an active light above, it is a relatively undemanding task. When a wanderer first arrives, they feel a rush of calmness, relief, and relaxedness. However, this is short-lived because after 3 minutes of arriving, wanderers reported seeing shadows in their peripheral vision, and feeling a sense of everlasting dread. After 10 minutes of staying here, wanderers often report hearing whispers behind the walls or whispers one far away, often they are unintelligible or negative, and also a looming sense of anxiety, and lastly a sense of someone being near. If they stay for 1 hour, hallucinations of all type become prevalent, scratches appear on the body, and the first signs of [[Phenomenon 35|Roth Syndrome]] appear. Staying longer is thought to make these symptoms more ubiquitous, although it is mostly unknown what happens if the wanderer survives this. Reportedly, wanderers who stay in '''The Mental Concrete''' for too long have reportedly turned into a [[Phenomenon 35|Wretch]] or most commonly Concretelurkers due to the level lacking any food and water to survive. [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]] have been reported several times in '''The Mental Concrete''', because of the lack of food, they are known to be more aggressive than in other levels. They have heightened hearing, meaning wanderer's loud noises can potentially trigger the [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]]. The numbers are growing, yet what balances out the wretch population because is the fact that they soon die from starvation and dehydration of the sub-layer. Reportedly when one wanders too deep in, they often notice that the concrete is starts to decay, and as the deeper they go, the decay seems to worsen, but no one has traveled too far due to the toxins causing lung diseases and the limited reports. Meaning that the level is speculated to be somehow infinite. Due to this, its not recommended to wander deeper areas of the sub-layer. This is the most isolated area in '''Level 960'''. It is impossible to survive for long term. To escape, a wanderer has to find a spiral upward staircase usually from the deeper areas, leading one to [[Level 26]]. =Entities= ==Concretelurkers== ===Description & Behaviour=== {{Entity Class|no=N/A|Hab=Level 960|Agg=5|Int=4|Dan=5}} {{Blurimage|caption=A composite drawing of a Concretelurker.|file=Carpet ambusher.jpg}} '''Concretelurkers''', taxonomically ''Proceriatas esseyus'', takes on an appearance of a long and multi-colored humanoid-like being, with having an extra set of arms and legs, with a total of 8 limbs. Their limbs are relatively long, which also extend rapidly to as far as 6 meters. Their facial features resemble sets of deformed ears, mouth, nose, and eyes. Most '''Concretelurkers''' are fully blind, but they have extremely enhanced hearing, rendering encounters as mainly fatal. They are physically stronger and faster than humans, even being stronger than some entities. They can walk fast, especially when searching for food. They can randomly ambush or surprise a wanderer, and sometimes they also hunt entities that reside the level. Almost all '''Concretelurker''' attacks are fatal. It is common that they ambush when inside of carpet, ceiling or even hollow walls. They are rumored that wanderers or entities that has been exposed to the concrete dust, has turned into a '''Concretelurker'''. ===Biology=== ''Proceriatas esseyus'' are in the family Cygnthyorphidia and in the genus ''Proceriatas'' which indicates a tripartite mandible and more than two sets of limbs. The organism possesses dual pectoral limb pairs connected by an elongated trapezius-like muscle. The fungal growths on its head can also split into two, providing a complete chamber for consumption of food. ===Discovery=== It remains unknown on whom discovered the existence of Concretelurkers. They have always been colloquial in conversation, and it is unknown who mentioned it first; it has always been common knowledge that they exist. ==Inhabitants== Other than '''Concretelurkers''', other entities that are reportedly to be present in the level are [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 2|Windows]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], [[Entity 4|Female Deathmoths]], [[Entity 5|Clumps]], [[Entity 6|Dullers]], and [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]]. =Colonies and Outposts= Due to the nature of this level, establishing colonies and outposts is seemingly possible, but albeit a Sisyphean task. =Entrances & Exits= ===Entrances=== *Attempting to noclip in a concrete pillar in [[Level 1]] has a high chance of sending one here; *Attempting to noclip the walls in [[Level 208]] will often lead one here; *Although inconsistent, noclipping in [[Level 315]] occasionally sends one here; *Although inconsistent, sleeping in [[Level 570]] occasionally sends one here; *Glitching through a floor in [[Level 450.1]] can send one here, although inconsistent; *Rarely, noclipping through any doors in [[Level 740]] sends one here; *Although inconsistent, random Noclipping in [[Level 959|▇▇▇▇▇▇▇▇]] can send one here; *Breaking and opening a crack and large enough to enter in [[Level 497]] leads one here, as one goes inside the level through a broken part, the level will repairs itself, which one cannot go back where they came from; *Digging down through the snow in [[Level 151]] has a small chance of coming across a large crack of the level leading one here, as one goes inside the level through a broken part, the level will repairs itself, which one cannot go back where they came from; *Noclipping through a floor in [[Level 201]] has a chance of sending one here. ===Exits=== *Entering a vent in '''The Residual Space''' will lead one to [[Level 19.2]]; *Noclipping into a extremely rare steel surface in '''The Residual Space''' will send one to [[Level 828]]; *Finding a crawlspace in '''The Decaying Maze''' will lead to [[Level 412]]; *Random Noclipping in '''The Concrete Office''' often leads one to a random level; *Finding a rare upward spiraling staircase in '''The Mental Concrete''' will often leads to [[Level 26]]; *Entering any cracks in '''Level 960''' wil lead one to [[The Void]]; *Random Noclipping anywhere occasionally sends one to those 3 sub layers, excluding '''The Mental Concrete''', but frequently leads one to [[The Void]]. Random Noclipping in '''The Mental Concrete''' will always send one to [[The Void]]; *Walls can sometimes have cracks with a pitch black void, breaking and jumping in it sends one to [[The Void]]. *Finding an extremely rare exit door will lead straight to [[Level 961]]. ----
